---
title: "Nasdaq-100 Historical Performance Dashboard"
subtitle: "Unified Dataset Explorer & Analytics"
author: "Antigravity Stock Dataset Builder"
date: "2026-06-30"
format:
  html:
    theme:
      dark: darkly
      light: flatly
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show Python Code"
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: full
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
from IPython.display import display, HTML

# Load the unified historical dataset
df = pd.read_csv("downloads/nasdaq100_historical.csv")
df['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Date'])
```

## 📊 Dataset Overview

We have successfully built a unified historical dataset containing daily stock price data for the constituents of the **Nasdaq-100** index.

```{python}
#| label: overview-stats
#| echo: false
total_rows = len(df)
unique_tickers = df['Ticker'].nunique()
min_date = df['Date'].min().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
max_date = df['Date'].max().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

kpi_html = f"""
<div style="display: flex; gap: 15px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin: 20px 0;">
    <div style="flex: 1; min-width: 200px; padding: 20px; border-radius: 12px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b 0%, #0f172a 100%); border: 1px solid #334155;">
        <div style="font-size: 0.85rem; color: #94a3b8; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase;">Total Records</div>
        <div style="font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 700; color: #f8fafc; margin-top: 5px;">{total_rows:,}</div>
        <div style="font-size: 0.8rem; color: #64748b; margin-top: 5px;">Total daily price rows</div>
    </div>
    <div style="flex: 1; min-width: 200px; padding: 20px; border-radius: 12px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b 0%, #0f172a 100%); border: 1px solid #334155;">
        <div style="font-size: 0.85rem; color: #94a3b8; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase;">Tickers Represented</div>
        <div style="font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 700; color: #38bdf8; margin-top: 5px;">{unique_tickers}</div>
        <div style="font-size: 0.8rem; color: #38bdf8; margin-top: 5px;">Nasdaq-100 constituents</div>
    </div>
    <div style="flex: 1; min-width: 200px; padding: 20px; border-radius: 12px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b 0%, #0f172a 100%); border: 1px solid #334155;">
        <div style="font-size: 0.85rem; color: #94a3b8; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase;">Date Range</div>
        <div style="font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; color: #f8fafc; margin-top: 10px;">{min_date} to {max_date}</div>
        <div style="font-size: 0.8rem; color: #64748b; margin-top: 5px;">Up to 10 years of trading history</div>
    </div>
</div>
"""
display(HTML(kpi_html))
```

---

## 🚀 Relative Performance Comparison (Magnificent Seven)

This chart compares the cumulative return of the **Magnificent Seven** tech giants starting from July 2016. A `$100` investment at the beginning of the period is tracked over time.

```{python}
#| label: relative-performance
mag7_tickers = ['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'GOOGL', 'AMZN', 'NVDA', 'META', 'TSLA']
df_mag7 = df[df['Ticker'].isin(mag7_tickers)].copy()

# Pivot data to get Close prices aligned by Date
df_pivot = df_mag7.pivot(index='Date', columns='Ticker', values='Close').dropna()

# Calculate cumulative returns (normalized to $100 starting value)
df_normalized = (df_pivot / df_pivot.iloc[0]) * 100

fig_cum = go.Figure()
colors = {
    'AAPL': '#a3a3a3',   # Silver/Gray
    'MSFT': '#00a4ef',   # Blue
    'GOOGL': '#ea4335',  # Red
    'AMZN': '#ff9900',   # Orange
    'NVDA': '#76b900',   # Green
    'META': '#0668e1',   # Royal Blue
    'TSLA': '#cc0000'    # Deep Red
}

for ticker in mag7_tickers:
    if ticker in df_normalized.columns:
        fig_cum.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=df_normalized.index,
            y=df_normalized[ticker],
            mode='lines',
            name=ticker,
            line=dict(color=colors.get(ticker, '#ffffff'), width=2)
        ))

fig_cum.update_layout(
    title='Growth of $100 Investment (Since July 2016)',
    xaxis_title='Date',
    yaxis_title='Investment Value (USD)',
    template='plotly_dark',
    height=600,
    hovermode='x unified',
    yaxis=dict(type='log')  # Use log scale since Nvidia growth is massive
)
fig_cum.show(config={'responsive': True})
```

---

## 📈 Apple Inc. (AAPL) Performance Detail

Since Apple was our starting stock, here is its standalone 10-year closing price trajectory.

```{python}
#| label: aapl-detail
df_aapl = df[df['Ticker'] == 'AAPL'].sort_values(by='Date').reset_index(drop=True)

fig_aapl = go.Figure()
fig_aapl.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df_aapl['Date'],
    y=df_aapl['Close'],
    mode='lines',
    name='AAPL Close Price',
    line=dict(color='#06b6d4', width=2),
    fill='tozeroy',
    fillcolor='rgba(6, 182, 212, 0.08)'
))

fig_aapl.update_layout(
    title='AAPL Historical Stock Price',
    xaxis_title='Date',
    yaxis_title='Stock Price (USD)',
    template='plotly_dark',
    height=450,
    margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=60, b=40)
)
fig_aapl.show(config={'responsive': True})
```

---

## 🔮 Stock Price Forecasting (1-Year Projection)

This interactive chart uses a statistical trend projection (fitted on the last 2 years of trading history) to forecast the stock price 1 year (252 trading days) into the future. Select a stock from the dropdown menu to see its prediction along with a **95% confidence interval** boundary representing standard volatility.

```{python}
#| label: stock-forecasting
import numpy as np

forecast_tickers = ['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'NVDA', 'AMZN', 'GOOGL', 'META', 'TSLA']
fig_forecast = go.Figure()

buttons = []
total_tickers = len(forecast_tickers)

for i, ticker in enumerate(forecast_tickers):
    df_t = df[df['Ticker'] == ticker].sort_values(by='Date').reset_index(drop=True)
    df_fit = df_t.tail(500).copy()
    
    x_fit = np.arange(len(df_fit))
    y_fit = df_fit['Close'].values
    slope, intercept = np.polyfit(x_fit, y_fit, 1)
    
    residuals = y_fit - (slope * x_fit + intercept)
    std_err = np.std(residuals)
    
    last_date = df_fit['Date'].max()
    future_dates = pd.date_range(start=last_date + pd.Timedelta(days=1), periods=365)
    future_trading_dates = future_dates[future_dates.dayofweek < 5][:252]
    
    x_pred = np.arange(len(df_fit), len(df_fit) + len(future_trading_dates))
    y_pred = slope * x_pred + intercept
    
    y_upper = y_pred + 1.96 * std_err
    y_lower = np.maximum(y_pred - 1.96 * std_err, 0)
    
    is_visible = (i == 0)
    
    # Trace 0: Historical Close
    fig_forecast.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=df_t['Date'].tail(500),
        y=df_t['Close'].tail(500),
        mode='lines',
        name=f'{ticker} Historical',
        line=dict(color='#06b6d4', width=2),
        visible=is_visible
    ))
    
    # Trace 1: Forecasted Trend
    fig_forecast.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=future_trading_dates,
        y=y_pred,
        mode='lines',
        name=f'{ticker} Forecast',
        line=dict(color='#e11d48', width=2, dash='dash'),
        visible=is_visible
    ))
    
    # Trace 2: Upper Bound
    fig_forecast.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=future_trading_dates,
        y=y_upper,
        mode='lines',
        name='Upper 95% Bound',
        line=dict(color='rgba(225, 29, 72, 0.2)', width=1),
        visible=is_visible,
        showlegend=False
    ))
    
    # Trace 3: Lower Bound
    fig_forecast.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=future_trading_dates,
        y=y_lower,
        mode='lines',
        name='Lower 95% Bound',
        line=dict(color='rgba(225, 29, 72, 0.2)', width=1),
        fill='tonexty',
        fillcolor='rgba(225, 29, 72, 0.05)',
        visible=is_visible,
        showlegend=False
    ))
    
    visibility_mask = [False] * (total_tickers * 4)
    visibility_mask[i*4 : (i+1)*4] = [True] * 4
    
    button = dict(
        label=ticker,
        method="update",
        args=[
            {"visible": visibility_mask},
            {"title": f"1-Year Price Projection & 95% Confidence Interval: {ticker}"}
        ]
    )
    buttons.append(button)

fig_forecast.update_layout(
    updatemenus=[
        dict(
            active=0,
            buttons=buttons,
            direction="down",
            pad={"r": 10, "t": 10},
            showactive=True,
            x=0.02,
            xanchor="left",
            y=1.15,
            yanchor="top",
            font=dict(color='#000000')
        )
    ],
    title=f"1-Year Price Projection & 95% Confidence Interval: {forecast_tickers[0]}",
    xaxis_title="Date",
    yaxis_title="Stock Price (USD)",
    template="plotly_dark",
    height=600,
    hovermode="x unified",
    margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=100, b=40)
)
fig_forecast.show(config={'responsive': True})
```

---

## 🎨 Advanced Market Analytics & Visualizations

This section presents two advanced analytical visualizations designed to uncover correlations and risk-reward profiles across the Nasdaq-100 constituents.

### 1. Daily Return Correlation Matrix (Heatmap)
This interactive heatmap illustrates the correlation of daily percentage returns between the **Magnificent Seven** stocks plus **Netflix (NFLX)** over the last 2 years. Higher values (closer to 1.0) represent stocks that move in tandem, highlighting sector-wide trends.

```{python}
#| label: correlation-heatmap
# Pivot the close prices of Mag7 + NFLX
analysis_tickers = ['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'GOOGL', 'AMZN', 'NVDA', 'META', 'TSLA', 'NFLX']
df_analysis = df[df['Ticker'].isin(analysis_tickers)].copy()
df_pivot_returns = df_analysis.pivot(index='Date', columns='Ticker', values='Close').pct_change().dropna()

# Compute correlation matrix
corr_matrix = df_pivot_returns.corr()

# Plot heatmap
fig_corr = px.imshow(
    corr_matrix,
    text_auto=".2f",
    color_continuous_scale="RdBu",
    zmin=-1,
    zmax=1,
    title="Daily Stock Return Correlation Matrix (2-Year)",
    labels=dict(color="Correlation Coefficient")
)
fig_corr.update_layout(
    template="plotly_dark",
    height=550,
    margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=60, b=40)
)
fig_corr.show(config={'responsive': True})
```

### 2. Risk vs. Reward Profile (Annualized Volatility vs. Annualized Return)
This scatter plot maps the annualized return against the annualized volatility (risk) for the **Top 15 constituents** of the index. Bubbles are sized relative to the stock's average daily trading volume, helping visualize where different assets sit on the risk-reward spectrum.

```{python}
#| label: risk-reward-scatter
top15 = ['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'NVDA', 'AMZN', 'GOOGL', 'META', 'TSLA', 'AVGO', 'PEP', 'COST', 'CSCO', 'ADBE', 'AMD', 'NFLX', 'TMUS']
df_top15 = df[df['Ticker'].isin(top15)].copy()

# Compute returns
df_top15['Return'] = df_top15.groupby('Ticker')['Close'].pct_change()

# Aggregate stats over the last 2 years
latest_2y_date = df['Date'].max() - pd.Timedelta(days=365 * 2)
df_recent_top15 = df_top15[df_top15['Date'] >= latest_2y_date]

stats = df_recent_top15.groupby('Ticker').agg(
    Avg_Return=('Return', 'mean'),
    Vol_Return=('Return', 'std'),
    Avg_Volume=('Volume', 'mean')
).reset_index()

# Annualize stats (assuming 252 trading days)
stats['Annualized_Return'] = stats['Avg_Return'] * 252 * 100
stats['Annualized_Volatility'] = stats['Vol_Return'] * np.sqrt(252) * 100

fig_scatter = px.scatter(
    stats,
    x="Annualized_Volatility",
    y="Annualized_Return",
    text="Ticker",
    size="Avg_Volume",
    color="Ticker",
    labels={
        "Annualized_Volatility": "Risk (Annualized Volatility %)",
        "Annualized_Return": "Reward (Annualized Return %)",
        "Avg_Volume": "Avg Daily Volume"
    },
    title="Risk vs. Reward Profile: Top 15 Nasdaq-100 Constituents (2-Year)"
)

fig_scatter.update_traces(textposition="top center", marker=dict(line=dict(width=1, color='White')))
fig_scatter.update_layout(
    template="plotly_dark",
    height=600,
    showlegend=False,
    margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=60, b=40)
)
fig_scatter.show(config={'responsive': True})
```

---

## 🗂️ Sample Data Explorer (Latest 10 Days)

Here are the latest daily records for the top 5 components by weight.

```{python}
#| label: data-table-explorer
#| echo: false
top5_tickers = ['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'NVDA', 'AMZN', 'GOOGL']
latest_dates = sorted(df['Date'].unique())[-10:]

df_explorer = df[(df['Ticker'].isin(top5_tickers)) & (df['Date'].isin(latest_dates))].copy()
df_explorer = df_explorer.sort_values(by=['Date', 'Ticker'], ascending=[False, True])

df_explorer['Date'] = df_explorer['Date'].dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
df_explorer['Open'] = df_explorer['Open'].map('${:,.2f}'.format)
df_explorer['High'] = df_explorer['High'].map('${:,.2f}'.format)
df_explorer['Low'] = df_explorer['Low'].map('${:,.2f}'.format)
df_explorer['Close'] = df_explorer['Close'].map('${:,.2f}'.format)
df_explorer['Volume'] = df_explorer['Volume'].map('{:,}'.format)

table_html = df_explorer.to_html(index=False, classes="table table-hover table-striped")
display(HTML(table_html))
```

---

*Dashboard generated dynamically using Quarto. Stock data extracted from Nasdaq historical quotes database.*
